**MILO_OFFICIAL_SCRIPT_v1**

seed_id: seed-083
edit_level: polish

script:
- setup: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."
- punchline: "We now have shared custardy."

changes_made: Trimmed the opening "When" — "When my wife and I divorced" drops in too slowly. "My wife and I divorced" puts you in the scenario in four words. Everything else was already correct. The punchline is untouchable and I didn't touch it.

caption_notes: Two cards. Setup: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop." The em-dash is a built-in visual pause — let it breathe on screen. Punchline: "We now have shared custardy." That word needs its own card and the full 3+ seconds to land.

timing_notes: Setup delivered with mild exasperation — this is a man explaining his divorce the way he'd explain a parking ticket. Not a tragedy, just a fact. 0.5s pause before punchline. Punchline flat and resigned. Do NOT punch "custardy" — any emphasis draws attention to the wordplay and deflates it. It lands harder when delivered like he's reading from a court document.

tts_notes: "custardy" — TTS should parse this correctly since it rhymes with "custody." If the voice model mangles it: phonetic fallback KUSS-ter-dee.

quality_flag: strong

### seed-083

**RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1**

**Primary Cast:**

| Role | Cast | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| Divorced Husband / Narrator | Jerry Gold — Classic Dad (adapted: Exasperated Divorced Co-Owner) | The setup requires someone who explains his divorce the way he'd explain a bureaucratic inconvenience — mild exasperation, resigned acceptance, no real bitterness. Jerry's "straight-man with absurd punchline" archetype is the mechanism here. He delivers "shared custardy" like he's reading from the actual settlement document. He has filed the paperwork. He is maintaining. |
| Ex-Wife (background visual anchor) | Zoe Sparks — Goofball Neighbor (adapted: Thriving Co-Owner, Other Side of the Tape) | Zoe on the other side of whatever visual divide the concept uses. She is doing well. Her half looks better. Her energy (undefeated, bubbly) is the visual counterweight to Jerry's resigned acknowledgment. The contrast between her half and his half tells the story before anyone speaks. |
| Judge (optional supporting, if courtroom concept) | Noah Check — Principal persona (adapted: Procedural Family Court Judge) | Noah's "compliance comedy" archetype makes him the judge who issued this ruling with complete bureaucratic seriousness and no awareness that "shared custardy" is a pun. He gaveled it in. He is proud of the phrasing. He thinks this is a well-worded ruling. |

**Adaptation Notes:**
- Jerry: No ring. Rumple the wardrobe slightly — this man has been through something and is maintaining. Not defeated; just processed.
- Zoe: Whatever apron, smock, or register-adjacent prop matches the setting. Her side of the divide should have more color, better organization, maybe a plant. This is non-negotiable for the visual contrast.
- Noah (if used): Judicial robes or official attire. He sits elevated. He has a gavel. He is performing authority correctly and without any sense of irony.
- Iris direction: The visual divide between Jerry's half and Zoe's half is the hook. Blue tape, a split sign, contrasting lighting — establish that divide in the first 1.5 seconds before anyone speaks. The pun has to live in the visuals too.
- Family believability: Jerry (white male, ~37) + Zoe (white female, ~27) = plausible former married couple.

**Shot Intent:**
- **Setup:** Jerry to camera, mid-exasperation. One hand gesturing vaguely at the divided situation behind him. Zoe visible in the background on her side, visibly fine. The divide is present and prominent.
- **Punchline:** Hold on Jerry. Resigned. Flat. "We now have shared custardy." He says it like citing a case number. Zoe's reaction — a small wave, a delighted expression — lands as visual confirmation on the other side of the divide.

### seed-083

**IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v1**

seed_id: seed-083
concepts_count: 3
Riley casting honored: Jerry Gold (Classic Dad) narrates. Zoe Sparks (Goofball Neighbor) as ex-wife. Noah Check (Principal) as judge — optional, courtroom concepts only.

---

**Concept A — "The Divided Shop"**
Style: Split pudding shop, two ex-spouses operating their halves, sitcom energy
Duration: 9.2s — Needs room for the setup's specificity before the pun lands.

Master video prompt:
"Colorful pudding shop interior divided by blue tape, cartoon 2.5D, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, rumpled teal jacket, white apron) on left. Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, neat white apron, fairy lights on her side) on right. Sign sawed in half above. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.0s): HOOK — The Divided Shop
A bright, cheerful pudding shop interior with pastel walls and warm lighting. Down the exact center of the tiled floor runs a thick strip of blue painter's tape, dividing the shop perfectly in half. On the left side stands Jerry Gold — a middle-aged man with brown hair, handlebar mustache, slightly rumpled teal jacket under a white apron. No wedding ring. He leans against his counter with arms crossed, surveying his half with resigned acceptance. His display case has puddings arranged in neat but joyless rows. On the right side stands Zoe Sparks — a young woman with orange-magenta two-tone hair in a ponytail, round teal glasses, teal jacket with star pin under a crisp white apron. She cheerfully arranges colorful pudding cups in her display case, which has fairy lights strung along the top, a small chalkboard menu with hand-drawn hearts, and a potted plant on the counter. Above them, the shop sign reads "PUDDING PALACE" — but it has been literally sawed in half down the middle, each piece hanging at a slightly different angle. The tape line runs through counter, floor, and ceiling. Jerry's side has no plant.

Scene 2 (2.0–5.6s): SETUP — The Situation
Same divided pudding shop. Jerry Gold on his side (brown hair, mustache, rumpled teal jacket, apron) faces the viewer, one hand gesturing behind him at the divided shop with weary exasperation. A pudding cup sits in his other hand. His expression is the face of a man explaining a parking ticket — mildly inconvenient, fully processed, no longer angry about it. Behind the tape line on her side, Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta ponytail, teal glasses, neat apron) serves a blurred customer with a bright smile, animated hand gestures, and obvious charm. Her cash register has a small handwritten sign. Jerry's register has no sign. The blue tape line is prominent between them. The lighting is slightly warmer on Zoe's side.
Caption: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."

Scene 3 (5.6–6.1s): THE BEAT
Closer framing on Jerry Gold at his counter. Rumpled teal jacket, apron, pudding cup in hand. He sighs — a visible exhale, shoulders dropping slightly. The blue tape is at the edge of frame. He is about to name this arrangement the way he'd cite a case number.

Scene 4 (6.1–9.2s): PUNCHLINE — The Arrangement
Same divided pudding shop. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, rumpled jacket, apron) looks at the viewer with deadpan acceptance, holding the pudding cup slightly raised like a tired toast to his new reality. Through the tape divide, Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta ponytail, teal glasses, apron) has turned toward Jerry. She is grinning, both hands clasped near her chin in delight — she genuinely finds the arrangement charming. She gives him a small wave across the tape. Jerry does not wave back. Above them, the split "PUDDING PALACE" sign hangs crookedly. Jerry's half of the shop is in cooler lighting. Zoe's half has the fairy lights twinkling. The visual contrast is immediate and deeply unfair to Jerry.
Caption: "We now have shared custardy."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.0s no text | 2.0–5.6s setup | 6.1–9.2s punchline
Voice: Jerry mild exasperation, explaining bureaucracy. 0.5s pause. Punchline flat, resigned, like reading a court document. Do NOT emphasize "custardy."
Risk: LOW. Single location, clear visual gag. Fallback: Jerry and Zoe at opposite ends of a kitchen table with pudding cups, tape line down the middle.
Jerry hook: "The divided shop is immediately funny on mute. Zoe thriving while Jerry endures tells the whole story before anyone speaks. The sign sawed in half is the cherry."
Vince: 9:16, 9.2s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+Zoe wave+split sign, Scene 1 divided shop reveal], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.3s]

---

**Concept B — "The Courtroom Ruling"**
Style: Family court hearing, Noah delivers the ruling, Jerry and Zoe receive it differently
Duration: 9.8s — Three-person courtroom needs room to breathe.

Master video prompt:
"Cartoon family courtroom, vertical 9:16, 2.5D illustration, clean bold lines. Noah Check (short hair, rectangular glasses, dark suit under judicial robes) at elevated bench with gavel. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, rumpled teal jacket, no ring) at left table. Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, teal jacket with star pin) at right table. Large ceramic custard bowl with spoon on courtroom floor between them — Exhibit A. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.0s): HOOK — The Courtroom
A cartoon family courtroom with wooden paneling and fluorescent lighting. At the elevated judge's bench sits Noah Check — a sturdy, no-nonsense man with short cropped hair, rectangular glasses, wearing a dark suit under black judicial robes. He holds a wooden gavel and surveys the proceedings with absolute seriousness. He is proud of this case. Below him at separate wooden tables: on the left, Jerry Gold — brown hair, handlebar mustache, slightly rumpled teal jacket, no wedding ring. He sits with one elbow on the table, chin in his hand, the posture of exhausted acceptance. On the right, Zoe Sparks — orange-magenta two-tone hair, teal glasses, teal jacket with a star pin. She sits perfectly upright, hands folded, serene. Between the two tables on the courtroom floor sits a comically large ceramic bowl of bright yellow custard pudding, a serving spoon sticking straight up out of it. A small placard taped to the bowl reads "EXHIBIT A." Nobody acknowledges this is unusual.

Scene 2 (2.0–5.8s): SETUP — The Ruling
Same courtroom. Noah Check at the bench (glasses, robes, gavel) reads from a document with bureaucratic concentration, one finger tracing the text. He is performing authority correctly. Jerry Gold at his table (mustache, rumpled teal jacket) has slumped further — both elbows on the table, hands clasped in front of his mouth. He stares at the custard exhibit with the expression of a man processing paperwork for a life event. His shoes may be slightly wet from custard that has seeped toward his table. Zoe Sparks at her table (teal glasses, teal jacket, star pin) nods along with small encouraging nods, one hand resting on a small potted plant she brought from home. The custard bowl gleams under fluorescent lights.
Caption: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."

Scene 3 (5.8–6.3s): THE PAUSE
Closer framing on Jerry Gold at his table. Rumpled jacket, no ring. He looks directly at the viewer. Flat resignation. The custard bowl visible at the bottom of frame. He's about to explain the arrangement.

Scene 4 (6.3–9.8s): PUNCHLINE — The Filed Paperwork
Same courtroom. Jerry Gold at his table (mustache, rumpled jacket) looks at the viewer with bureaucratic acceptance — he has signed the documents, he is maintaining. Behind him, Noah Check at the bench (glasses, robes) has set down his gavel with both hands flat on the bench, satisfied. Noah is proud of the ruling's phrasing. He thinks "shared custardy" is well-worded. He has no awareness it is a pun. Across the aisle, Zoe Sparks (teal glasses, teal jacket) has both hands clasped near her face, beaming. She finds the arrangement delightful. Between the tables, the large custard bowl has been visually split — a clean line through the middle, one half tilting toward Jerry, the other toward Zoe. A second spoon has appeared. Everything is procedurally correct.
Caption: "We now have shared custardy."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.0s no text | 2.0–5.8s setup | 6.3–9.8s punchline
Voice: Jerry mild exasperation. 0.5s pause. Punchline flat, citing a case number.
Risk: LOW. Fallback: Jerry at a kitchen table with a legal document and a pudding cup.
Jerry hook: "Three cast members, each doing different emotional work. Noah proud of a pun he doesn't know is a pun. Zoe delighted. Jerry enduring. The custard exhibit is the prop that ties it all together."
Vince: 9:16, 9.8s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+split custard+Noah proud+Zoe beaming, Scene 1 courtroom establish], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.3s]

**Concept C [SURREAL] — "The Custard Flood"**
Style: Normal courtroom that melts into pudding when the gavel falls
Duration: 10.0s

surreal_element: Noah's gavel strike cracks the judge's bench, which is actually a giant custard bowl. Pudding floods the courtroom. Jerry stands in it, resigned. Zoe scoops it with her hands. Noah is proud.
Weirdness budget: 7/10 — Controlled absurdity, not chaos. Everyone treats the pudding flood as standard procedure.

Scene 1 (0.0–2.5s): HOOK — Normal Courtroom (Grounded)
A normal cartoon courtroom — wooden paneling, fluorescent lights, American flag in the corner. Noah Check at the judge's bench — short hair, rectangular glasses, dark suit under black robes. Standard gavel. Jerry Gold at the left table — brown hair, mustache, rumpled teal jacket, arms crossed. Zoe Sparks at the right table — orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, teal jacket, serene. Everything is ordinary. But on Noah's bench, next to his papers, sits a single pudding cup with a spoon in it. Nobody has mentioned it.

Scene 2 (2.5–5.5s): SETUP — The Ruling (Reality Softens)
Same courtroom, but the edges are wrong. Noah's wooden judge's bench is warping — its surface rounding and glazing over, the wood grain becoming ceramic, the color shifting to pale yellow. It is becoming a giant custard bowl. Noah (glasses, robes) still reads from his document with perfect bureaucratic composure, unbothered that his bench is now slightly sticky. His wooden gavel has become a large silver serving spoon, which he holds naturally. Jerry at his table (mustache, rumpled jacket) has noticed the custard seeping across the floor toward his shoes. He has chosen not to comment. Zoe at her table (teal glasses) hasn't noticed — she's reading a small book titled "Starting Over: A Guide to Thriving." The American flag in the corner has begun to droop, as if made of caramel.
Caption: "My wife and I divorced, and the judge made us split everything evenly — including our pudding shop."

Scene 3 (5.5–6.5s): TRANSFORMATION BEAT
Full transformation. Noah Check stands inside what is now a building-sized ceramic custard bowl — the bench is gone. He is waist-deep in thick yellow pudding, robes floating on the surface. He holds the spoon-gavel high above his head with both hands, expression concentrated, authoritative. He brings it down. CRACK — the bowl splits down the middle, a clean line from rim to rim. Yellow custard floods outward in a wave. Jerry at his table braces — one hand gripping the table edge, expression unchanged. He expected this. Zoe at her table spreads her arms wide, welcoming the wave, laughing. The courtroom walls begin dripping yellow.

Scene 4 (6.5–10.0s): PUNCHLINE — Shared Custardy
The courtroom is flooded waist-deep with thick yellow custard. The cracked bowl has split into two perfect halves — one tilting toward Jerry, one toward Zoe. Jerry Gold stands in custard (mustache, rumpled jacket soaked), looking at the viewer with flat resignation. A pudding cup floats past him. He does not reach for it. On the other side, Zoe Sparks (teal glasses, ponytail) scoops custard with both bare hands and tastes it, grinning, pudding on her chin. Between them in the background, Noah Check stands in the cracked bowl — robes drenched, spoon-gavel resting on his shoulder — looking down at his work with profound professional satisfaction. He did this. The ruling was correct. The American flag drips. Everyone is calm.
Caption: "We now have shared custardy."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.5s no text | 2.5–5.5s setup | 6.5–10.0s punchline
Transformation beat: 5.5–6.5s — spoon-gavel cracks the bowl-bench, custard floods courtroom.
Voice: Jerry resigned. 0.5s pause. Punchline flat bureaucratic. Chaos not acknowledged.
Risk: MEDIUM. Fluid dynamics + transformation. Fallback: hard cut from normal courtroom to fully-flooded courtroom — skip morph.
Jerry hook: "This one's weird on purpose. The AI will make it weirder. That's the bit. Noah proudly standing in the wreckage of his own ruling is an image that haunts. Zoe eating the evidence is the closer."
Vince: 9:16, 10.0s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 flooded courtroom, Scene 3 transformation, Scene 1 normal courtroom], trims=[Scene 1 shorten to 1.5s]

- decision: GREENLIGHT
- confidence: High
- rationale: Concept A wins on first principles — the divided shop tells the entire joke before Jerry opens his mouth. Tape line, sawed sign, Zoe's side with fairy lights, Jerry's side without a plant. The visual contrast is immediate and the pun has a visual confirmation to land against. Concept B (courtroom) adds a third character and more setup time for no proportional gain. Concept C (custard flood) is too chaotic for a joke that's already doing everything it needs to do quietly.
- cost_note: Low. Single interior, two characters, one recurring prop (the tape line). The sawed PUDDING PALACE sign is the only render challenge — fallback to a hand-lettered split sign if AI gen struggles.
- must_keep: PUDDING PALACE sign sawed in half, two halves at different angles; Zoe's side warmer and better decorated than Jerry's; Jerry's flat resigned expression throughout; Zoe's wave in scene 4 with Jerry not waving back
